params.name: Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
params.content:
# Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies

A timeless recipe for soft, chewy chocolate chip cookies that are golden on the edges and gooey in the center.

## Ingredients

- 2 ¼ cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter, softened
- ¾ cup granulated sugar
- ¾ cup packed brown sugar
- 2 large eggs
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 2 cups semi-sweet chocolate chips

## Instructions

1. **Preheat** the oven to 375°F (190°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
2. **Combine dry ingredients:** In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
3. **Cream the butter and sugars:** In a large bowl, beat the softened butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar together until light and fluffy, about 3–4 minutes.
4. **Add eggs and vanilla:** Beat in the eggs one at a time, then mix in the vanilla extract until combined.
5. **Mix in dry ingredients:** Gradually add the flour mixture to the wet ingredients, mixing on low speed until just combined. Do not overmix.
6. **Fold in chocolate chips:** Stir in the chocolate chips with a spatula or wooden spoon.
7. **Scoop the dough:** Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
8. **Bake:** Bake for 9–11 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown but the centers still look slightly underdone.
9. **Cool:** Let the cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

## Tips

- **Chill the dough** for at least 30 minutes (or up to 24 hours) for thicker, chewier cookies.
- **Don't overbake!** The cookies will continue to firm up as they cool.
- **Storage:** Keep in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days.

## Yield

Makes approximately **48 cookies**.

Enjoy! 🍪

params.name: Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
params.content:
# Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies

A timeless recipe for soft, chewy chocolate chip cookies that are golden on the edges and gooey in the center.

## Ingredients

- 2 ¼ cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter, softened
- ¾ cup granulated sugar
- ¾ cup packed brown sugar
- 2 large eggs
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 2 cups semi-sweet chocolate chips

## Instructions

1. **Preheat** your oven to 375°F (190°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
2. **Combine dry ingredients:** In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
3. **Cream the butter and sugars:** In a large bowl, beat the softened butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar together until light and fluffy (about 2–3 minutes).
4. **Add eggs and vanilla:** Beat in the eggs one at a time, then mix in the vanilla extract until well combined.
5. **Mix in dry ingredients:** Gradually add the flour mixture to the wet ingredients, mixing on low speed until just combined. Do not overmix.
6. **Fold in chocolate chips:** Stir in the chocolate chips with a spatula or wooden spoon.
7. **Scoop the dough:** Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
8. **Bake:** Bake for 9–11 minutes, or until the edges are golden but the centers still look slightly underdone.
9. **Cool:** Let the cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

## Tips

- For extra flavor, use a mix of semi-sweet and dark chocolate chips.
- Chill the dough for 30 minutes before baking for thicker cookies.
- Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days.

---

**Yield:** ~48 cookies | **Prep Time:** 15 min | **Bake Time:** 10 min

params.name: Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
params.content:
# Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies

A timeless recipe for soft, chewy chocolate chip cookies that are golden on the edges and gooey in the center.

## Ingredients

- 2 ¼ cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter, softened
- ¾ cup granulated sugar
- ¾ cup packed brown sugar
- 2 large eggs
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 2 cups semi-sweet chocolate chips

## Instructions

1. **Preheat** your oven to 375°F (190°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
2. **Combine dry ingredients:** In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
3. **Cream the butter and sugars:** In a large bowl, beat the softened butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar together until light and fluffy (about 2–3 minutes).
4. **Add eggs and vanilla:** Beat in the eggs one at a time, then mix in the vanilla extract until well combined.
5. **Mix in dry ingredients:** Gradually add the flour mixture to the wet ingredients, mixing on low speed until just combined. Do not overmix.
6. **Fold in chocolate chips:** Stir in the chocolate chips with a spatula or wooden spoon.
7. **Scoop the dough:** Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
8. **Bake:** Bake for 9–11 minutes, or until the edges are golden but the centers still look slightly underdone.
9. **Cool:** Let the cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

## Tips

- For extra flavor, use a mix of semi-sweet and dark chocolate chips.
- Chill the dough for 30 minutes before baking for thicker cookies.
- Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days.

---

**Yield:** ~48 cookies | **Prep Time:** 15 min | **Bake Time:** 10 min

# Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies

A timeless recipe for soft, chewy chocolate chip cookies that are golden on the edges and gooey in the center.

## Ingredients

- 2 ¼ cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter, softened
- ¾ cup granulated sugar
- ¾ cup packed brown sugar
- 2 large eggs
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 2 cups semi-sweet chocolate chips

## Instructions

1. **Preheat** your oven to 375°F (190°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
2. **Combine dry ingredients:** In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
3. **Cream the butter and sugars:** In a large bowl, beat the softened butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar together until light and fluffy (about 2–3 minutes).
4. **Add eggs and vanilla:** Beat in the eggs one at a time, then mix in the vanilla extract until well combined.
5. **Mix in dry ingredients:** Gradually add the flour mixture to the wet ingredients, mixing on low speed until just combined. Do not overmix.
6. **Fold in chocolate chips:** Stir in the chocolate chips with a spatula or wooden spoon.
7. **Scoop the dough:** Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
8. **Bake:** Bake for 9–11 minutes, or until the edges are golden but the centers still look slightly underdone.
9. **Cool:** Let the cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

## Tips

- For extra flavor, use a mix of semi-sweet and dark chocolate chips.
- Chill the dough for 30 minutes before baking for thicker cookies.
- Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days.

## 🎄 Holiday Variation: Peppermint White Chocolate Cookies

Give your cookies a festive twist perfect for the holiday season!

### Ingredient Swaps

- Replace semi-sweet chocolate chips with **white chocolate chips**.
- Add **½ cup crushed candy canes** (about 6–8 candy canes).
- Substitute vanilla extract with **1 tsp peppermint extract**.

### Additional Steps

1. Fold in the white chocolate chips and crushed candy canes in step 6.
2. After baking, drizzle cooled cookies with melted white chocolate and sprinkle with extra crushed candy cane pieces for a stunning presentation.

### Holiday Tips

- Add a few drops of red or green food coloring to the dough for a fun pop of color.
- Package in decorative tins or cellophane bags for a perfect homemade holiday gift.
- Pair with a cup of hot cocoa or eggnog for the ultimate festive treat!

---

**Yield:** ~48 cookies | **Prep Time:** 15 min | **Bake Time:** 10 min

params.name: Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies
params.content:
# Classic Chocolate Chip Cookies

A timeless recipe for soft, chewy chocolate chip cookies that are golden on the edges and gooey in the center.

## Ingredients

- 2 ¼ cups all-purpose flour
- 1 tsp baking soda
- 1 tsp salt
- 1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter, softened
- ¾ cup granulated sugar
- ¾ cup packed brown sugar
- 2 large eggs
- 2 tsp vanilla extract
- 2 cups semi-sweet chocolate chips

## Instructions

1. **Preheat** the oven to 375°F (190°C). Line baking sheets with parchment paper.
2. **Combine dry ingredients:** In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda, and salt. Set aside.
3. **Cream butter and sugars:** In a large bowl, beat the softened butter, granulated sugar, and brown sugar together until light and fluffy (about 2–3 minutes).
4. **Add eggs and vanilla:** Beat in the eggs one at a time, then mix in the vanilla extract until combined.
5. **Mix in dry ingredients:** Gradually add the flour mixture to the wet ingredients, mixing on low speed until just combined. Do not overmix.
6. **Fold in chocolate chips:** Stir in the chocolate chips with a spatula or wooden spoon.
7. **Scoop dough:** Drop rounded tablespoons of dough onto the prepared baking sheets, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
8. **Bake:** Bake for 9–11 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown but the centers still look slightly underdone.
9. **Cool:** Let the cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

## Tips

- For extra flavor, use a mix of semi-sweet and dark chocolate chips.
- Chill the dough for 30 minutes before baking for thicker cookies.
- Store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 5 days.

**Yield:** ~48 cookies | **Prep Time:** 15 min | **Bake Time:** 10 min

  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</blueprint>

tools.name: set-alarm
tools.description:
Schedule an alarm to wake yourself up in the future.
Provide exactly one of "in" (a duration from now) or "at" (an absolute time).
When the alarm fires you will receive a prompt and can continue working.
Setting a new alarm replaces any previously scheduled one.

in.description: Duration from now expressed as "<number> <unit>", e.g. "30 seconds", "5 minutes", "2 hours".

at.description: Absolute ISO-8601 timestamp to wake at, e.g. "2026-06-04T18:00:00.000Z".

tools.name: get-current-date
tools.description: Get the current date and time as an ISO-8601 string.

timezone.description: Optional IANA timezone name. Defaults to the process clock when omitted.

tools.name: create
tools.description: Creates a new markdown document and adds it to the space.

tools.name: open
tools.description: Opens and reads the contents of a new markdown document.

doc.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

tools.name: update
tools.description: Applies a set of edits to the markdown document.

doc.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

oldString.description: The text to find in the document.

newString.description: The text to replace it with.

replaceAll.description: If true, replaces all occurrences. Defaults to false (first occurrence only).

edits.description: The edits to apply to the document. Each edit finds oldString and replaces it with newString.
